title: Asynchronous Architecture and Pattern Selection · Contract Lens: Temporal Coupling Decision
summary: A request path adds a broker without a latency or failure reason, hides synchronous coupling behind RPC messages, and uses one global queue for work distribution, broadcasting, and replay. Planned artifact: Add and verify the temporal coupling decision increment in pattern selection and coupling decision record. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Choose asynchronous messaging only when temporal decoupling, buffering, fan-out, failure isolation, or replay creates measurable value.
Plan browser models, TypeScript compilation, contract tests, disposable RabbitMQ integration, partition, restart, load, and production transfer gates.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: RabbitMQ and TypeScript Predict Temporal Coupling Decision · Theory Temporal Coupling Decision
steps.instruction:
theory case — A theory messaging team handles case 1571 in a canary consumer reading old and new envelope versions. Build deterministic TypeScript evidence to choose HTTP, work queue, pub/sub, or retained stream from explicit coupling needs; retain a screen-reader-readable delivery and ownership trace; then reuse one message identity with a different payload fingerprint. Browser code models pure messaging decisions only. amqplib, AMQP sockets, RabbitMQ, TLS, partitions, restarts, Docker, databases, external effects, load, upgrades, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates.

Before reading, predict which action preserves the Temporal Coupling Decision contract for the supplied data.
